content:
# Session 92 (iter-092) — review

## Metadata
- File: `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjInverse.lean` (SOLO `prove`, last file of the v4.31.0 migration route).
- Sorry: iter-091 left it **RED at 9**; iter-092 = **GREEN, 7 sorries** (closed 2 + restored green).
- `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjInverse` → **EXIT 0, 8565 jobs** (review-verified, foreground).
- 8 edits, 0 task_result written by prover (but it LANDED green, unlike iter-091).

## Headline
**iter-092 LANDED** (vs iter-091 non-landing RED). It (a) fixed the single iter-091 compile break, and
(b) closed the 2 Cone A unit-coherence legs. Net 9→7, file compiles.

### Closed this iter (all GENUINE — auditor + lvb confirmed, no cheats)
1. `tensorObj_left_unitor_image_collapse` (~L1722) — the iter-091 RED break (was L1743). **axis-9 fix**:
   `simpa only [Iso.app_hom, asIso_hom] using (...).right_triangle_components (...)` fails at reducible
   transparency on the `(forget ⋙ restrictScalars 𝟙).obj (unit) ≡ 𝟙_` unit-object spelling → replaced
   with `have h := ...; simp only [...] at h; exact h` (bare `exact` = default transparency). **New durable
   fact: axis-9 bites `right_triangle_components` too, not only iter-090's `toRingCatSheafHom` case.**
2. `tensorObj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify` (~L1801) — Cone A λ-leg. Ported v4.30 body: tensor-leg
   split (`tensorObjIsoOfIso_eq_comp`) → `tensorObj_left_unitor_naturality` slide → residual discharged by
   `tensorObj_left_unitor_image_collapse` via an axis-9 `congrArg` fold.
3. `pullbackUnitIso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker` (~L1881) — η-whisker leg. bridge-1 substitution +
   central-leg STU two-arg naturality (`sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_natural`) + `aesop_cat` for the routine
   `𝟙 ≫ f = f`. Central leg = already-proven naturality, no new coherence core.

## KEY NEW FINDING — B1 keystone WALLED on a dropped ROOT lemma (cross-file migration regression)
The prover attempted **`tensorObj_restrict_iso_eq_pullbackTensorMap`** (B1, the δ-conjugation keystone)
and WALLED it honestly (full diagnosis in the L817-836 comment). Root cause:
- The v4.30 proof (parent `117100c4`, ROOT `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:4678`) used
  **`pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse`** — the δ-conjugation **EQUATION**.
- The v4.31-migrated working-tree ROOT was **RESTRUCTURED and DROPPED** `pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse`
  (+ `deltaConjOfMuComparison`, `isIso_oplaxδ_of_conj`), replacing them with
  `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` — an `IsIso` **witness**, NOT the equation B1 needs.
- Confirmed: `pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse` exists ONLY in `117100c4:TensorObjSubstrate.lean:4678`
  (and 4748/4790/4806/4818 usages); absent from the entire working tree except B1's own dead comment.
- **The SAME dropped lemma is what K1 `pullbackTensorMap_isIso_of_isOpenImmersion` needs** (its v4.30 proof
  at `117100c4:TensorObjSubstrate.lean:4790` calls `pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse f M.val N.val`).

**Implication:** the prior plan ("K1 = in-place port from ROOT, blocked only by OOM/file-split") is
**incomplete** — K1 (and B1) are gated on a **ROOT-recovery** lane that re-ports the δ-collapse EQUATION
into the v4.31 working-tree ROOT FIRST. A file-split of K1 alone cannot proceed until ROOT re-exposes it.

## Remaining 7 sorries (all expected / honest comments; auditor + lvb agree)
| Decl | Line | Class | Gate |
|------|------|-------|------|
| `pullbackTensorMap_isIso_of_isOpenImmersion` (K1) | 807 | heavy + ROOT-gap | ROOT-recovery + file-split |
| `tensorObj_restrict_iso_eq_pullbackTensorMap` (B1) | 837 | ROOT-gap (WALLED iter-092) | ROOT-recovery (`pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse`) |
| `tensorObj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` | 982 | LIGHT recoverable | port from 117100c4 (independent) |
| `dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble` | 1511 | dual-unit FLANK (∞-source) | blueprint ∞-source first |
| `trivialisation_restrict_compat` | 2298 | telescope | rides B1 + flank |
| `dualUnitIso_dualIsoOfIso` | 2315 | dual-unit FLANK | blueprint ∞-source first |
| `exists_tensorObj_inverse` | 2372 | terminal | rides telescope |

## Audit findings (full reports linked; do NOT re-grind)
- **lean-auditor `iter092`** (`task_results/lean-auditor-iter092.md`): the 3 closed decls GENUINE (no cheat,
  no axiom leakage). "11 must-fix" = the 7 expected sorries + 4 excuse-comments (auditor rubric flags every
  `:= sorry` + `TODO(v4.31.0)` comment); **the ONE genuinely actionable doc finding** is a STALE docstring:
  `tensorObj_restrict_iso_eq_pullbackTensorMap` L800 still reads `**STATUS (iter-051): CLOSED at this level.**`
  while its body is now `sorry` (WALLED iter-092) — a doc-vs-code contradiction → prover/refactor to fix.
  4 majors = transitively-hollow private helpers (`tensorObj_unit_iso_restrict_compat(_inner)`,
  `dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat`, `tensorObj_unit_self_duality_collapse`) — known cascade, no misleading
  docstrings, consumers also sorried. 2 minors = legit linter suppressions (L309-310) + honest stub comment.
- **lean-vs-blueprint-checker `tensorobjinverse-iter092`** (`task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-tensorobjinverse-iter092.md`):
  **SOUND, 0 must-fix.** The 3 closed decls faithfully match richly-detailed blueprint blocks
  (`lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse`, `..._pullback_eq_sheafify`, `lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_*`);
  every `\lean{}` target for the file resolves; chapter is MORE than adequate (proof sketches anticipate the
  v4.31 transparency seams). 1 minor: cosmetic hom/inv slip in the displayed solved-form equation of
  `lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse` (chapter L9131 orients μ as `.hom` where solving for the unitor
  needs `μ.inv`); **the Lean statement is correct** — only the prose paraphrase is imprecise → `% NOTE` added.

## blueprint-doctor
Clean — no orphan chapters, no broken `\ref`/`\uses`, no new `axiom`.

## Convergence
**CONVERGING (route), with one corrected dependency.** iter-091 RED→iter-092 GREEN + 2 real eliminations
on the Cone A unit legs is genuine forward motion. The migration route (089 DualInverse 6→0, 090
TensorObjSubstrate 5→1, 091/092 TensorObjInverse 19→9→7) is closing. The corrected fact: K1 **and** B1 are
NOT pure in-file ports — they share a **ROOT-recovery** dependency (`pushforward_mu_appIso_collapse` dropped
from the v4.31 ROOT). Next iter should (a) light-sweep `tensorObj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` (independent),
and (b) open a ROOT-recovery lane for the dropped δ-collapse equation before re-attempting K1/B1.

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`, `lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse`: added `% NOTE:` flagging the
  L9131 display-equation hom/inv orientation slip (μ should be `.inv` in the solved-for-unitor form; the
  authoritative Lean statement `μ.hom ; ε▷ ; unitor = a.map λ` is correct). Per lvb minor finding.
- No `\leanok` overrides: `sync_leanok` (iter 92, sha 4d85c5c, +48/−0) correctly re-added the 48 markers the
  iter-091 RED cone had stripped; build EXIT 0 confirms. No laundering.
- No `\mathlibok` warranted (closed decls are project proofs, not Mathlib re-exports).
